title: davilla rugosa var. rugosa
intro: Davilla rugosa var. rugosa, also known as the Philippine tree or wild cat's claw, is a medicinal plant species that belongs to the family Opiliaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiparasitic activities. The health benefits of Davilla rugosa var. rugosa include its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and various gastrointestinal issues, as well as its potential to boost the immune system. Davilla rugosa var. rugosa can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in traditional medicine in Southeast Asia, particularly in the Philippines.
